# Candidate Characterization Report

This report summarizes probabilistic characterizations of topologically prioritized candidate missing planets. The outputs are prioritization aids, not detections.

## Run notes

- Catalog source: C:\Users\175199\OneDrive - Fundacion Universidad de las Americas Puebla\Topological-mapping-of-detection-bias-and-observational-incompleteness-in-exoplanet-catalogs\reports\imputation\PSCompPars_imputed_iterative.csv
- Validation completed in multiplanet mode.
- Candidate source: C:\Users\175199\OneDrive - Fundacion Universidad de las Americas Puebla\Topological-mapping-of-detection-bias-and-observational-incompleteness-in-exoplanet-catalogs\outputs\system_missing_planets\candidate_missing_planets_by_system.csv
- Predictions generated for 1144 prioritized candidates.

## Method

The module combines orbital/stellar candidate features, weighted analogs in the observed catalog, quantile regression for mass and radius, radius-class probabilities, and physical post-processing for density and detectability proxies.

## Interpretation warning

Predicted properties are conditional estimates under the observed catalog, feature engineering choices, imputation history, and discovery biases. They should be used to prioritize follow-up and stress-test candidates, not to claim confirmed planets.
